title: Quantum Noise Models

theory.introduction: Real quantum systems are affected by noise due to interaction with the environment. Noise introduces errors in quantum states, causing loss of coherence and incorrect computation results. Understanding noise is essential for designing robust quantum algorithms and error correction techniques.
key_points: Quantum states are fragile and easily affected by environment
Noise causes decoherence and incorrect measurement outcomes
Different types of errors affect amplitude and phase
Noise modeling helps design error correction techniques

bit_flip_error.description: Changes qubit state from |0⟩ to |1⟩ or vice versa.
bit_flip_error.analogy: Similar to classical bit flip error

phase_flip_error.description: Changes phase of qubit without changing probability.

bit_phase_flip.description: Combination of bit flip and phase flip errors.

depolarizing_noise.description: Randomly applies X, Y, or Z error with certain probability.
depolarizing_noise.effect: Qubit moves toward completely mixed state

decoherence.description: Loss of quantum information due to environmental interaction.
decoherence.importance: Major challenge in real quantum computers

bloch_sphere_effects.depolarization: State moves toward center of Bloch sphere

circuit_examples.name: Bit Flip Noise
circuit_examples.description: Apply X error with probability p

summary: Quantum noise models describe how quantum states are affected by environmental interactions. Understanding noise is critical for building reliable quantum systems.

simulation.expected_result: Both |0⟩ and |1⟩ outcomes demonstrate noise effect
try_this: H creates superposition, then X simulates a bit-flip error, Z simulates a phase-flip error
Notice how errors accumulate on the quantum state
Measurement shows the statistical outcome — error correction aims to undo these before measurement

quiz.question: What does a bit-flip error do to a qubit?
options: Changes the phase of the qubit
Flips the state from |0⟩ to |1⟩ or vice versa
quiz.answer: Flips the state from |0⟩ to |1⟩ or vice versa
quiz.explanation: A bit-flip error is equivalent to an unintended X gate, flipping the qubit state.

quiz.question: Which gate represents a phase-flip error?
quiz.explanation: A phase-flip error flips the sign of the |1⟩ amplitude, exactly what the Z gate does.

quiz.question: What is decoherence?
options: Loss of quantum information due to environmental interaction
quiz.answer: Loss of quantum information due to environmental interaction
quiz.explanation: Decoherence occurs when a quantum system interacts with its environment, destroying its quantum properties.

quiz.question: What does depolarizing noise do to a qubit on the Bloch sphere?
options: Moves it to the north pole
Moves it toward the center of the sphere
quiz.answer: Moves it toward the center of the sphere
quiz.explanation: Depolarizing noise pushes the qubit state toward the maximally mixed state at the center of the Bloch sphere.
